2026-03-02T09:23:46.7377590Z   Mocha/Chai patterns to Vitest patterns:
2026-03-02T09:23:46.7377963Z   - Replace 'import { assert } from "chai"' with vitest imports
2026-03-02T09:23:46.7378397Z   - Replace 'import { Context } from "mocha"' with VitestTestContext
2026-03-02T09:23:46.7378873Z   - Update beforeEach/afterEach to use arrow functions with (ctx) param
2026-03-02T09:23:46.7379343Z   - Replace 'this.currentTest' with ctx in Recorder constructor
2026-03-02T09:23:46.7381088Z   - Replace 'createRecorder(this)' with 'createRecorder(ctx)'
2026-03-02T09:23:46.7381370Z

2026-03-02T09:23:46.7381952Z   the combined git add command at end of file
2026-03-02T09:23:46.7382156Z 
2026-03-02T09:23:46.7382244Z - using-azure-identity.md:
2026-03-02T09:23:46.7382579Z   - Update DefaultAzureCredential chain to list all 7 credential types
2026-03-02T09:23:46.7383044Z     (was only listing 2: EnvironmentCredential + ManagedIdentityCredential)
2026-03-02T09:23:46.7383533Z   - Update InteractiveBrowserCredential from outdated 'implicit grant flow'
2026-03-02T09:23:46.7383963Z     to current 'Authorization Code Flow with PKCE'
2026-03-02T09:23:46.7384356Z   - Remove stale NOTE claiming InteractiveBrowserCredential only works in
2026-03-02T09:23:46.7384730Z     browser (it supports Node.js as well)

2026-03-02T09:23:46.7463257Z  
2026-03-02T09:23:46.7463434Z  ### InteractiveBrowserCredential
2026-03-02T09:23:46.7463671Z  
2026-03-02T09:23:46.7463925Z -The `InteractiveBrowserCredential` follows the [implicit grant
2026-03-02T09:23:46.7464512Z -flow](https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/active-directory/develop/v2-oauth2-implicit-grant-flow)
2026-03-02T09:23:46.7465116Z -which enables authentication for clients that run completely in the browser. It
2026-03-02T09:23:46.7465633Z -is primarily useful for single-page web applications (SPAs) which need to
2026-03-02T09:23:46.7466076Z -authenticate to access Azure resources and APIs directly.
2026-03-02T09:23:46.7466876Z +The `InteractiveBrowserCredential` follows the [Authorization Code Flow with PKCE](https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/active-directory/develop/v2-oauth2-auth-code-flow)
2026-03-02T09:23:46.7467740Z +which enables authentication for clients running in a browser (via popups or redirects)
2026-03-02T09:23:46.7468297Z +or on Node.js (by opening a browser window). It is primarily useful for single-page
2026-03-02T09:23:46.7468820Z +web applications (SPAs) and desktop/native applications that need to authenticate
2026-03-02T09:23:46.7469273Z +users interactively to access Azure resources and APIs.
2026-03-02T09:23:46.7469564Z  
2026-03-02T09:23:46.7470018Z -To use this credential successfully, your app registration will need to be
2026-03-02T09:23:46.7470519Z -configured with both the **Access tokens** and **ID tokens** options checked under
2026-03-02T09:23:46.7470952Z -**Implicit grant** in the **Authentication** page.
2026-03-02T09:23:46.7471384Z +On Node.js, the credential opens a browser window and listens for a redirect response
2026-03-02T09:23:46.7471934Z +from the authentication service. On browsers, it authenticates via popups by default,
2026-03-02T09:23:46.7472425Z +or via redirects if the `loginStyle` option is set to `"redirect"`.
2026-03-02T09:23:46.7472748Z  
2026-03-02T09:23:46.7473035Z -You will also need to add a redirect URI in the **Redirect URIs** section of the
2026-03-02T09:23:46.7473523Z -**Authentication** page for your app registration. The redirect URI must point
2026-03-02T09:23:46.7476292Z -to the URI of your web application. You must also make sure to specify the same
2026-03-02T09:23:46.7476842Z -URI in the `redirectUri` field of the `InteractiveBrowserCredentialOptions` when
2026-03-02T09:23:46.7477284Z -creating an `InteractiveBrowserCredential`.
2026-03-02T09:23:46.7477698Z +To use this credential, your app registration must have a redirect URI configured
2026-03-02T09:23:46.7478207Z +in the **Authentication** page. For desktop apps on Node.js, configure a "Mobile
2026-03-02T09:23:46.7478720Z +and desktop applications" redirect endpoint. For browser-based apps, configure a
2026-03-02T09:23:46.7479147Z +single-page application (SPA) redirect endpoint.
2026-03-02T09:23:46.7479421Z  
2026-03-02T09:23:46.7479865Z -> NOTE: At this time, this credential can only be used in the browser but
2026-03-02T09:23:46.7480577Z -> Node.js support will be added in the future (see issue [#4774](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-js/issues/4774)).
2026-03-02T09:23:46.7481249Z +You will need to specify the `redirectUri` in the `InteractiveBrowserCredentialOptions`
2026-03-02T09:23:46.7481812Z +when creating the credential, matching the URI registered in your app registration.
2026-03-02T09:23:46.7482205Z  
2026-03-02T09:23:46.7482512Z  ### DeviceCodeCredential
2026-03-02T09:23:46.7482716Z  
2026-03-02T09:23:46.7483038Z @@ -314,7 +313,12 @@ which tries each of the following credential types in order until one of them
2026-03-02T09:23:46.7483447Z  succeeds:
2026-03-02T09:23:46.7483608Z  
2026-03-02T09:23:46.7483770Z  - `EnvironmentCredential`
2026-03-02T09:23:46.7484016Z +- `WorkloadIdentityCredential`
2026-03-02T09:23:46.7510437Z  - `ManagedIdentityCredential`
2026-03-02T09:23:46.7510720Z +- `VisualStudioCodeCredential`
2026-03-02T09:23:46.7510975Z +- `AzureCliCredential`
2026-03-02T09:23:46.7511207Z +- `AzurePowerShellCredential`
2026-03-02T09:23:46.7511629Z +- `AzureDeveloperCliCredential`
2026-03-02T09:23:46.7511886Z  
2026-03-02T09:23:46.7512200Z  This credential type is ideal when one of the credentials in the chain will work
2026-03-02T09:23:46.7512730Z  in the current environment, whether it's your local development or a production
2026-03-02T09:23:46.7513106Z --
